title: Managing breakpoints
text: Managing breakpoints Add, remove, or edit multiple breakpoints at specific locations in your ProcScript modules. You can also specify conditional breakpoints, which means that the application should break only if a certain condition occurs. Editing breakpoints Refer to Trigger / Module when editing breakpoints. ProcScript Module and Line Refer to the following topics: ProcScript Module for breakpoints ProcScript Line for breakpoints Location list The Location list displays the structure name, ProcScript module name, and line number of breakpoints. Structure Specifiy the name of the component or global ProcScript that contains the breakpoint. If you are adding a new breakpoint, enter the name of the structure in which to set the breakpoint. Types For a new breakpoint, select a component or global ProcScript from the drop-down list. The selected value must be consistent with the Structure name field. That is, if you choose Component , the Structure name should be the name of a component. Conditions Specify a conditional expression that can be handled by the $condition ProcScript function. If you want the breakpoint to occur only if a specific condition occurs, enter a conditional expression. For example, $status=-1 , or $procerror=-11 , or any other condition that evaluates to TRUE or FALSE. The debugger does not compile the conditional expression, so you cannot use compile time constants, including ProcScript error constants. You must enter literal values, such as the -1 and -11 in the preceding example. For more information, see $condition . Buttons Use the breakpoint buttons, which include: Remove —remove the breakpoint highlighted in the Location list. Add —add a new breakpoint as specified in the Details fields. OK —accept the modifications you have made using this form Cancel —exit this form without applying the changes. Related Links Breakpoints A breakpoint is a marked code line, trigger, error, exception, or condition that forces the application or component to break and the debugger to take control of execution.
